hero.art_alt: A great blue heron at the edge of a marsh at dawn, a picture of calm and watchfulness.
hero.h1: How many companies are holding your personal information right now?
hero.lede: You have no idea. Agreely changes that: a receipt for every 'yes,' and the power to take your consent back whenever you want.

problem.label: The problem
problem.heading: You tick 'I agree' a hundred times a year. Afterward, you have nothing.
problem.lede: No receipt. No way to see who looked at your information. And when you change your mind, you have to hope that someone, somewhere, honors your choice.
problem.c1_p: You agree, the screen closes, and you are left with no proof of what you said yes to.
problem.c2_p: There is no way to know who inside the company looked at your information, or when.
problem.c3_p: Changing your mind should be simple. In practice, your 'no' often gets lost along the way.

gifts.label: What you get
gifts.heading: Three powers, one receipt that fits in your hand.
gifts.lede: No jargon, no empty promises. Here, plainly, is what Agreely puts in your hands.
gifts.g1_p: Every time you agree to share a piece of information, Agreely hands you a receipt. It stays on your phone. No one, not even the company, can quietly change it later.
gifts.gv1_seal: Sealed on your phone: any change shows.
gifts.g2_h: See who looked at your information
gifts.g2_p: A simple log answers one question: who looked at my information, and when? You see it without revealing who you are anywhere else.
gifts.g3_p: Changed your mind? Take your consent back with a tap, using your fingerprint or your face. From the very next time, the company gets a 'no.'
gifts.g3_caveat: Withdrawal applies going forward: it stops future uses, not the ones already made.
gifts.g4_p: A receipt can be checked by an independent tool, a bit like a referee who isn't on anyone's side. You don't have to trust Agreely: the math does the work.

app.label: Your app
app.heading: All of this fits in your pocket.
app.lede: Open my.agreely.ca and add it to your home screen: it installs and works like a real app, with no app store in the way.
app.b1_p: You get in with your fingerprint or your face. Nothing to remember, nothing to lose.
app.b2_p: No name, no email address, no phone number. Agreely never asks for them.
app.b3_p: It installs in one tap and works even offline, when the network drops.
app.shot_alt: The Agreely citizen app showing the list of consents and their receipts.

why.label: Why this is good for you
why.heading: You finally move to the side that has the proof.
why.lede: In every agreement, the company kept the record, and you kept nothing. Agreely flips that. You are no longer the only person in the room without a receipt.
why.i1: You are on equal footing. You hold the same proof the company holds, right in your pocket.
why.i2: You can see clearly. Who looked at your information, and when: it's there in black and white.
why.i3: You decide. A yes, a no, a change of heart: your choice is honored and proven.

manifeste.label: Take action
manifeste.heading: Ask the companies you know to be 'Agreely.'
manifeste.lede: The fastest way to change things is to ask. When enough people ask for a real consent receipt, companies start offering one.
manifeste.box_hint: You can edit it before you send it.
manifeste.message:
Hello,

As a customer, protecting my personal information matters to me. Quebec's Law 25 gives me the right to clear consent, and I want to be able to keep proof of it.

Does your company offer (or plan to offer) verifiable consent receipts, where I can see what I agreed to, who accessed my information, and easily withdraw my consent?

Tools like Agreely (agreely.ca) make this possible. I'd really love to see you offer it.

Thanks!
manifeste.mail_subject: Do you offer verifiable consent receipts?
manifeste.thanks: Thank you! Your support stays on your device. Share it to win over one more company.
manifeste.note: No account, no server: signing just keeps a note on your device. Nothing is sent to or stored by Agreely.

honesty.label: Let's be honest
honesty.heading: Let's be honest about what Agreely does, and does not, do.
honesty.c1_p: Agreely stores neither your name nor your email. We keep the proof of your consents, not your information itself.
honesty.c2_h: No one can link you from one company to another
honesty.c2_p: Each company sees only its own relationship with you. Agreely gives no one a way to follow you from one place to the next.
honesty.c3_p: We do not promise to stop leaks or hackers. We give you proof, visibility and control. That is different, and it is honest.

faq.label: Your questions
faq.heading: Your questions, in plain words.
faq.lede: Short answers to the questions we hear most.
faq.q1: Is this just another way to grab my data?
faq.a1: No, it's the opposite. Agreely asks you for no name, no email, no address. We keep the proof of your consents, not your information. The point is to give you more, not to take from you.
faq.a2: No. The citizen app is free for you. Companies are the ones who pay to offer real consent receipts.
faq.q3: Is my information on a blockchain?
faq.a3: No. Only a seal made from your receipt goes there, like a wax seal: it proves the receipt hasn't changed, without anyone being able to trace it back to you. Your information itself never leaves your device.
faq.q4: How can a 'no' actually be honored?
faq.a4: When you withdraw your consent, the company gets a 'no' permanently tied to your receipt. From the next time on, it knows the permission is gone, and you keep the proof. Withdrawal applies going forward, not to what has already been done.
faq.q5: What if I lose my phone?
faq.a5: You get a 12-word recovery phrase when you create your account. Keep it somewhere safe: it lets you restore everything on a new device.
faq.a6: It's Quebec's law on the protection of personal information. Among other things, it gives you the right to clear, free and informed consent, and you can ask for proof of it. To learn more, see the Agreely documentation.
faq.disclaimer: For information only, not legal advice.

cta.heading: A small gesture. Real weight.
cta.copy: Open your app, or ask a company to offer real consent receipts. Both matter.
